feedback.text: Dear European Commission. Thank you for the opportunity to contribute to the consultation on the Digital Fairness Act. This initiative is a vital step toward ensuring fairness, transparency, and consumer protection in the digital environment. I strongly support the Acts objectives to enhance consumer rights and promote a fair digital marketplace. In addition to the proposed measures, I urge the inclusion of a specific provision to address the preservation of "killed games" and the prevention of future game terminations, as outlined below. Proposed Addition: Preserving "Killed Games" and Preventing Future Losses I propose that the Digital Fairness Act include a provision requiring digital service providers, particularly in the gaming industry, to implement measures that preserve access to video games that are at risk of being "killed" (i.e., rendered unplayable due to server shutdowns, license expirations, or other decisions by publishers) and to establish safeguards to prevent such losses in the future. This could include mandating the release of server software, source code, or other tools necessary for players to maintain access to games offline or through community-hosted servers after official support ends. Reason for Inclusion: Video games are not merely entertainment products; they are cultural artifacts that hold significant artistic, historical, and social value. When games are "killed," entire communities lose access to shared experiences, creative works, and digital heritage. For example, multiplayer games like The Crew or Concord have been shut down, erasing players investments of time, money, and emotional engagement. This practice undermines consumer rights, as players are often left with no recourse to access content they have legally purchased. By including a provision to preserve games, the Digital Fairness Act can protect consumers rights to access their purchased digital goods, promote cultural preservation, and foster a sustainable digital ecosystem. Such a measure would also encourage innovation, as developers and communities could build upon existing games, ensuring their longevity and continued relevance. This addition aligns with the Acts goals of enhancing consumer protection and ensuring fairness in digital markets. It would set a global precedent for recognizing video games as cultural assets worthy of preservation, while empowering players and safeguarding their digital rights. Thank you for considering this proposal. I believe it will strengthen the Digital Fairness Act and contribute to a more equitable digital future. Sincerely, Marcus.
